source_quote: The total amount of alleviation of crowding with the sequential (6 weeks for each archwire) application of 0.014-in and 0.016-in archwires was 4.07 mm in the NiTi group and 3.58 mm in the CuNiTi group. No statistically significant three-way interaction was observed between week x diameter x application types in terms of LII (p > 0.05).

The reduction in LI was highest in the first month in both the groups, 2.88 +/- 0.31 mm in heat-activated NiTi and 2.33 +/- 0.66 mm in superelastic NiTi. RM ANOVA: Grouping F=0.152, p=0.69; Time interval*Grouping F=0.9, p=0.45.

The current study showed 2.56 mm and 2.87 mm reduction in the irregularity index in the A-NiTi and Cu-NiTi groups, respectively. Before treatment: A-NiTi 8.73 (2.22), Cu-NiTi 8.34 (2.15). After treatment: A-NiTi 6.17 (1.82), Cu-NiTi 5.47 (1.69). P=0.065.

Pain score: A-NiTi mean (SD) = 5.84 (2.04), Cu-NiTi mean (SD) = 6.16 (2.23), P=0.487, test statistic = -0.697. Note: pain measured using modified McGill pain questionnaire and pictorial VAS; scale appears to be 0-10 not 0-100 mm.
Analgesic intake: A-NiTi Yes=15 (34.1%), No=29 (65.9%) out of 44; Cu-NiTi Yes=8 (18.6%), No=35 (81.4%) out of 43. P=0.102, test statistic=2.682.
